security.issue: 'getPaymentMethod' accesses $_POST/$_GET/$_REQUEST without nonce verification — CSRF attack allows unauthorized state changes via forged requests
security.fix: Add wp_verify_nonce($_POST['_wpnonce'], 'action_name') before processing any user input; use wp_nonce_field() in forms

performance.issue: 'getPaymentMethod' has no obvious performance issues for its current scope (source: pagarme-payments-for-woocommerce.json)
performance.fix: Consider adding transient caching if the function's output becomes expensive as data volume grows

wp_api_usage.issue: 'getPaymentMethod' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: pagarme-payments-for-woocommerce.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit
wp_api_usage.fix: Use do_action() / apply_filters() to expose extension points where appropriate for plugin compatibility

code_quality.issue: 'getPaymentMethod' (14 lines, source: pagarme-payments-for-woocommerce.json) lacks explicit error handling — silent failures make debugging and monitoring difficult
code_quality.fix: Add WP_Error returns for failure conditions: if (empty($result)) { return new WP_Error('no_result', __('Operation failed', 'plugin-slug')); }

dependency_integrity.issue: 'getPaymentMethod' has no problematic dependency patterns detected (source: pagarme-payments-for-woocommerce.json)
dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs

i18n.issue: 'getPaymentMethod' i18n handling is acceptable (source: pagarme-payments-for-woocommerce.json) — verify text domain consistency across the plugin
i18n.fix: Ensure text domain matches the plugin slug defined in plugin header; use esc_html__() (not esc_html(__()) ) for the late-escaping pattern

accessibility.issue: 'getPaymentMethod' does not produce HTML output — accessibility dimension not applicable (source: pagarme-payments-for-woocommerce.json)
accessibility.fix: No changes needed for accessibility in this non-rendering function

critique.key_observation: 'getPaymentMethod' accesses $_POST/$_GET/$_REQUEST without nonce verification — CSRF attack allows unauthorized state changes via forged requests

critique.summary: Function 'sort_posts' has 2 critical issue(s) (sql_safety, security) requiring immediate remediation before deployment.

wpcs_compliance.issue: WPCS coding style is acceptable in 'sort_posts' but lacks PHPDoc completeness
wpcs_compliance.fix: Ensure @param types and @return type are documented

sql_safety.issue: $wpdb database queries in 'sort_posts' execute without $wpdb->prepare() — SQL injection vulnerability allows attackers to manipulate database queries via unsanitized input
sql_safety.fix: Wrap all dynamic values in $wpdb->prepare() with typed placeholders: $wpdb->prepare('SELECT * FROM %s WHERE id = %d', $table, absint($id))

security.issue: 'sort_posts' accesses $_POST/$_GET/$_REQUEST without nonce verification — CSRF attack allows unauthorized state changes via forged requests
security.fix: Add wp_verify_nonce($_POST['_wpnonce'], 'action_name') before processing any user input; use wp_nonce_field() in forms

performance.issue: N+1 query pattern in 'sort_posts': $wpdb query inside a loop causes O(n) database calls — degrades under load with many items
performance.fix: Extract IDs in one query, then batch-load in a single IN() query outside the loop: $wpdb->get_results($wpdb->prepare('SELECT * FROM table WHERE id IN (' . implode(',', array_map('absint', $ids)) . ')'))

wp_api_usage.issue: 'sort_posts' uses raw $wpdb queries for post data instead of WP_Query — bypasses WordPress caching, filters, and multisite table prefix support
wp_api_usage.fix: Replace raw SQL with WP_Query: $query = new WP_Query(['post_type' => 'post', 'posts_per_page' => -1]); $posts = $query->posts;

code_quality.issue: 'sort_posts' (28 lines, source: yith-woocommerce-ajax-search.json) lacks explicit error handling — silent failures make debugging and monitoring difficult
code_quality.fix: Add WP_Error returns for failure conditions: if (empty($result)) { return new WP_Error('no_result', __('Operation failed', 'plugin-slug')); }

dependency_integrity.issue: 'sort_posts' has no problematic dependency patterns detected (source: yith-woocommerce-ajax-search.json)
dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs

i18n.issue: 'sort_posts' i18n handling is acceptable (source: yith-woocommerce-ajax-search.json) — verify text domain consistency across the plugin
i18n.fix: Ensure text domain matches the plugin slug defined in plugin header; use esc_html__() (not esc_html(__()) ) for the late-escaping pattern

accessibility.issue: 'sort_posts' renders HTML (source: yith-woocommerce-ajax-search.json) — verify semantic structure uses appropriate HTML5 elements
accessibility.fix: Use semantic HTML: <nav>, <main>, <article>, <section> where appropriate; avoid div-soup for content that has semantic meaning

critique.key_observation: 'sort_posts' accesses $_POST/$_GET/$_REQUEST without nonce verification — CSRF attack allows unauthorized state changes via forged requests

critique.summary: Function 'ot_type_post_select' has 1 critical issue(s) (security) requiring immediate remediation before deployment.

wpcs_compliance.fix: Ensure @param types and @return type are documented

sql_safety.fix: If database access is added later, always use $wpdb->prepare() with proper placeholders

security.issue: extract() in 'ot_type_post_select' can overwrite arbitrary PHP variables from user-supplied arrays — privilege escalation risk
security.fix: Replace extract($_POST) with explicit variable assignments: $field = sanitize_text_field($_POST['field'] ?? '')

performance.issue: 'ot_type_post_select' performs potentially expensive operations in a loop without caching (source: option-tree.json)
performance.fix: Cache expensive computed values using wp_cache_set()/wp_cache_get() with appropriate cache group and expiry

wp_api_usage.issue: 'ot_type_post_select' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: option-tree.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit
wp_api_usage.fix: Use do_action() / apply_filters() to expose extension points where appropriate for plugin compatibility

code_quality.issue: 'ot_type_post_select' (42 lines, source: option-tree.json) lacks explicit error handling — silent failures make debugging and monitoring difficult
code_quality.fix: Add WP_Error returns for failure conditions: if (empty($result)) { return new WP_Error('no_result', __('Operation failed', 'plugin-slug')); }

dependency_integrity.issue: 'ot_type_post_select' has no problematic dependency patterns detected (source: option-tree.json)
dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs

i18n.fix: Ensure text domain matches the plugin slug defined in plugin header; use esc_html__() (not esc_html(__()) ) for the late-escaping pattern

accessibility.issue: 'ot_type_post_select' renders form inputs without associated <label> elements — screen readers cannot identify form fields, failing WCAG 2.1 success criterion 1.3.1
accessibility.fix: Add <label for='field_id'>Field Name</label> before each <input id='field_id'>, or use aria-label attribute: <input aria-label='Field Name'>

critique.key_observation: extract() in 'ot_type_post_select' can overwrite arbitrary PHP variables from user-supplied arrays — privilege escalation risk
